## RESEARCH COVERAGE SUMMARY

### Code Generation Models Documented
- **30+ models** across closed-source (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) and open-source (Meta, DeepSeek, etc.)
- **Performance metrics** (HumanEval, MBPP, SWE-bench, APPS scores)
- **Model specializations** (SQL, Python, JavaScript, etc.)
- **Size comparisons** (from 3.8B to 405B parameters)
- **Cost analysis** ($0-$0.015 per 1K tokens)

### Benchmarks & Evaluation
- **15+ major benchmarks** covered (HumanEval, MBPP, SWE-bench, APPS, BigCodeBench, etc.)
- **Evaluation metrics** (CodeBLEU, pass@k, ICE-Score, etc.)
- **Benchmark comparisons** (difficulty, size, purpose)
- **Leaderboards** (current top performers)

### Research Papers
- **30+ peer-reviewed papers** (2023-2026)
- **Key findings** extracted and summarized
- **Citation information** for all papers
- **Research methodologies** explained

### Tools & Frameworks (20+)
- **IDE Integration** (GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Continue.dev, Codeium, TabNine)
- **Local Model Runners** (Ollama, LM Studio, LocalAI, vLLM)
- **Project Generators** (GPT Engineer, Aider, OpenDevin)
- **LLM Frameworks** (LangChain, LiteLLM, RAG, Hugging Face)
- **Infrastructure** (AWS, Google Cloud, Azure, managed services)

### Integration Guides
- **VSCode** - Step-by-step installation and configuration
- **JetBrains IDEs** - IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, Rider setup
- **Custom Integration** - Python code examples with APIs
- **Docker** - Containerization example
- **Local Deployment** - Using Ollama, vLLM, LocalAI

### Code Examples
- **100+ code snippets** throughout documents
- **Prompt templates** for common tasks
- **Fine-tuning examples** (LoRA, QLoRA)
- **Integration patterns** (service classes, API calls)
- **Configuration examples** (JSON, YAML, Python)

### Performance Data
- **Latency benchmarks** (500ms-5s per generation)
- **Throughput measurements** (100-8,000 tokens/sec)
- **Cost analysis** (self-hosted vs API)
- **Memory requirements** (FP16 and quantized)
- **Hardware recommendations** (RTX 4090, A100, etc.)

## HOW TO ACCESS & USE

### Direct File Access
All files are located in: `/home/shuvam/codes/LLM-Whisperer/`

**Command to view all files**:
```bash
ls -lh /home/shuvam/codes/LLM-Whisperer/CODE_GENERATION*.md
```

### Reading Order (Recommended)

**For Quick Overview** (15 minutes):
1. CODE_GENERATION_PACKAGE_README.md (sections 1-4)
2. CODE_GENERATION_RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md (Executive Findings)

**For Detailed Understanding** (2-3 hours):
1. CODE_GENERATION_PACKAGE_README.md (all sections)
2. CODE_GENERATION_RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md (all sections)
3. CODE_GENERATION_COMPREHENSIVE_GUIDE.md (Parts 1-6)

**For Complete Reference** (4-6 hours):
1. Start with Package README for navigation
2. Read Comprehensive Guide end-to-end
3. Use Research Index as reference

**For Specific Tasks**:
- **Model Selection**: Research Index Section 1 + Comprehensive Part 1
- **Benchmarking**: Research Index Section 2 + Comprehensive Part 5
- **Integration**: Comprehensive Part 8 + Research Index Section 5
- **Security**: Comprehensive Part 6 + Research Summary
- **Fine-tuning**: Comprehensive Part 9
- **Performance**: Comprehensive Part 10 + Research Index Section 6
